summary:
Held 2 of today's 10 biggest UK/EU movers.

• 4 upside misses — AKZA.AS +20%, CON.DE +4%, DTG.DE +3%, BRBY.L +3%. These cleared the universe screen but no strategy picked them up; the morning brief either filtered them on a catalyst/RSI/earnings rule or had nothing positive to trigger an entry.

• 4 downside moves (KGF.L -4%, CAP.PA -4%, TEP.PA -3%, UMG.AS -3%) are unreachable on a long-only basket — no mechanism to express a short or pair-trade.

• Most instructive miss: AKZA.AS (+19.54%) — Nippon Paint & Sherwin-Williams disclosed a joint €73/share cash bid (~39% premium); AkzoNobel rejected it for its $25B all-stock Axalta merger, but the competing offer set a hard valuation floor. Why we passed: Outside any active strategy's universe — AKZA.AS is a large-cap Amsterdam coatings name. The +19.5% gap was M&A-driven (competing €73/share cash bid disclosed after hours); momentum signals cannot fire on a pre-open disclosure.

top_movers.catalyst: Nippon Paint & Sherwin-Williams disclosed a joint €73/share cash bid (~39% premium); AkzoNobel rejected it for its $25B all-stock Axalta merger, but the competing offer set a hard valuation floor.
top_movers.miss_reason: Outside any active strategy's universe — AKZA.AS is a large-cap Amsterdam coatings name. The +19.5% gap was M&A-driven (competing €73/share cash bid disclosed after hours); momentum signals cannot fire on a pre-open disclosure.
top_movers.suggestion: Add a pre-market M&A news scanner that ingests regulatory disclosures (AFM/SEC) and flags names with a disclosed cash bid price >5% above prior close; route them into an event-driven catalyst basket for opening-range entry.

top_movers.catalyst: Continued re-rating on EU-US tariff deal (May 20, 15% cap) plus CEO's €300M refund disclosure; World Cup kit-sales tailwind and consensus price-target upgrade to ~€196 adding momentum.
top_movers.miss_reason: Outside any active strategy's universe — ADS.DE is a large-cap DAX consumer-discretionary name, not a typical momentum/mean-reversion candidate. The +5% move was the tail end of a multi-week macro re-rating (tariff relief), not a single-session gap that news-reactive filters would catch intraday.
top_movers.suggestion: Add a 'tariff-overhang-lift' watchlist for EU exporters with high disclosed US-tariff exposure (≥€200M); trigger macro-aligned entry when an EU-US trade accord is ratified and the name is within 15% of its 52-week low.

top_movers.catalyst: No single news catalyst — macro/sector re-rating of European auto cyclicals, likely amplified by Continental's ContiTech auction (Apollo/Bain final bids) nearing a buyer announcement at ~€4–5B, adding shareholder-return optionality.
top_movers.miss_reason: Outside any active strategy's universe. CON.DE (DAX large-cap, ~€14B) had its price history reset by the Sep-2025 Aumovio spin-off, likely disqualifying it from momentum/mean-reversion strategies that require an uninterrupted adjusted-close time series.
top_movers.suggestion: Add a post-spin-off re-admission rule: re-qualify spin-adjusted tickers 180 days after ex-date once the adjusted-close series spans ≥252 trading days, so CON.DE-type names re-enter the universe with clean momentum signals.

top_movers.catalyst: EU Parliament/Council ratified Turnberry Agreement (~May 20) capping EU auto/truck tariffs at 15% vs threatened 25%, driving sector-wide relief rally (Stoxx Autos +2.6%); DTNA Q2 orders up 86% YoY adds recovery tailwind.
top_movers.miss_reason: Outside any active strategy's universe — DTG.DE is a ~€32B German commercial-vehicle large-cap. Move is macro/trade-policy driven (EU ratification removing 25% tariff threat); momentum/mean-reversion signals cannot anticipate event-driven tariff-relief gaps.
top_movers.suggestion: Add a trade-policy event layer: when EU-US deal ratification votes pass, screen EU auto/industrial names with US revenue exposure ≥30% for improving order data and admit them to a short-duration event-driven sleeve capped at 1% NAV.

top_movers.catalyst: Morgan Stanley upgraded BRBY to Overweight (PT GBP 13.50) post-FY26 results, citing adj. operating profit tripling to £160M and improving full-price sales mix as evidence of sustained turnaround execution.
top_movers.miss_reason: Outside any active strategy's universe. BRBY.L is a FTSE 100 luxury consumer-discretionary name. The +3.1% move was a sell-side re-rating event (Equal Weight→Overweight) arriving ~10 trading days after earnings, which pre-market momentum and mean-reversion signals cannot anticipate.
top_movers.suggestion: Extend the UK-EU universe to FTSE 100 consumer-discretionary names and add a broker-upgrade gate: if a tier-1 bank upgrades to Overweight within 10 sessions of an earnings release, allow a long entry at next open with a 1× ATR stop.

top_movers.catalyst: Goldman Sachs 3% voting-rights stake disclosure (filing dated ~May 25–27) plus Citigroup price-target raise to €80; continuation of post-Q2 guidance-raise rally with AI/power-electronics demand tailwind.
top_movers.miss_reason: Outside any active strategy's universe. IFX.DE is a €100B+ DAX large-cap up ~105% YTD and +18.7% on the week; macro-aligned was the designated strategy but likely passed due to extreme near-term extension (RSI overextension, gap-filter breach) rather than a fundamental screen failure.
top_movers.suggestion: Add a voting-rights / 13D disclosure trigger to macro-aligned: release the extension gate for names with a ≥3% institutional stake filing within the prior 3 sessions, capped at 0.5× normal position size to control overextension risk.

top_movers.catalyst: Ex-dividend date today (8.6p final dividend, ~3.0% of close) strips most of the decline mechanically; residual ~0.6% likely reflects cautious Q1 FY26/27 guidance ('tricky times ahead') from May 26 results.
top_movers.miss_reason: Outside any active strategy's universe. KGF.L is a UK mid-cap home improvement retailer (LSE ~£4B); the primary -3.0% move is mechanical ex-dividend stripping — a calendar-driven event no momentum or mean-reversion filter should treat as a tradeable signal.
top_movers.suggestion: Add ex-dividend flag to the daily mover screener: suppress names where |price decline| ≤ declared dividend amount, so mechanical strips don't surface as false-positive trade misses in post-trade review.

top_movers.catalyst: Capgemini's May 27 Capital Markets Day set 2025-28 revenue CAGR of +5.5-7.5% (incl. ~2pts M&A), below AI-acceleration expectations; shares gapped down ~4% on May 28 as worst CAC 40 performer.
top_movers.miss_reason: Outside any active strategy's universe — CAP.PA is a CAC 40 large-cap French IT-services name. Move was triggered by an after-hours CMD event (May 27), requiring pre-open positioning; post-open momentum or mean-reversion signals would miss the initial gap entirely.
top_movers.suggestion: Expand the news-reactive strategy's EU universe to include CAC 40 IT-services names and add a scheduled-event calendar gate: flag CMD/analyst-day names with gap-down ≥2% at open vs. prior close as eligible short entries.

top_movers.catalyst: Q1 2026 revenue fell 6.9% reported (–2.2% LFL) on EUR/USD headwinds and contract delays; Morgan Stanley cut target to €53 from €112 and Deutsche Bank to €70 from €105, continuing a post-earnings analyst downgrade cascade.
top_movers.miss_reason: Outside any active strategy's universe — TEP.PA is a mid-large-cap Euronext Paris BPO name in structural AI-disruption decline; today's –3.28% is a multi-day drift continuation from Q1 results and analyst re-ratings, not a single-session signal that momentum or gap filters would surface.
top_movers.suggestion: Add SBF 120 members to the tradable universe with ADV ≥ €15M and flag names where sell-side consensus PT has dropped ≥20% in 5 sessions as fade candidates; TEP.PA's downgrade cluster was visible two sessions before today's continuation leg.

top_movers.catalyst: Wells Fargo downgrade to Equal Weight and Wolfe Research PT cut €30→€27, amplified by Spotify's -44%-from-peak decline compressing value of UMG's pending ~€1.2B SPOT stake divestiture.
top_movers.miss_reason: Outside any active strategy's universe — UMG is a €34B Amsterdam media/entertainment large-cap. The move reflects analyst downgrades and Spotify peer-contagion, not a technical setup momentum or mean-reversion filters would surface.
top_movers.suggestion: (no actionable change — move is downside and bot is long-only; to catch analogues, a short-capable news-reactive layer could screen for ≥2 analyst cuts in 30 days on AEX large-caps, but requires short infrastructure not currently in scope)
